title: Azure Arc for Servers Heartbeat and Extension Inventory
app: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Cloud Services (Azure Monitor / Resource Graph export)
spl:
index=azure_monitor sourcetype="azure:arc:vm" earliest=-24h
| eval hb_age_h=(now()-last_heartbeat_epoch)/3600
| where hb_age_h>24 OR extension_count=0 OR isnull(extension_count)
| stats max(hb_age_h) as max_offline_h by resource_name
| sort -max_offline_h
description: Arc is the control path for Azure Policy, Update Management, and hybrid monitoring on HCI nodes. Stale heartbeats or missing extensions mean patches and guest configuration are not enforced, increasing security and uptime risk.
value: Arc is the control path for Azure Policy, Update Management, and hybrid monitoring on HCI nodes. Stale heartbeats or missing extensions mean patches and guest configuration are not enforced, increasing security and uptime risk.
implementation: (1) Export Arc-enabled machine inventory to Event Hub or blob and ingest via add-on; (2) normalize `last_status` to UTC; (3) alert when heartbeat older than 24h or expected extensions missing; (4) join with CMDB rack location for dispatch.
visualization: Table (stale Arc agents), Single value (machines without extensions), Map (site by resource group).

references.title: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Cloud Services

detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚ÄĘ Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Cloud Services (Azure Monitor / Resource Graph export).
‚ÄĘ Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=azure_monitor` `sourcetype="azure:arc:vm"` with fields `resource_name`, `last_heartbeat_epoch`, `agent_version`, `extension_count`.
‚ÄĘ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚ÄĒ Configure data collection
(1) Export Arc-enabled machine inventory to Event Hub or blob and ingest via add-on; (2) normalize `last_status` to UTC; (3) alert when heartbeat older than 24h or expected extensions missing; (4) join with CMDB rack location for dispatch.

Step 2 ‚ÄĒ Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=azure_monitor sourcetype="azure:arc:vm" earliest=-24h
| eval hb_age_h=(now()-last_heartbeat_epoch)/3600
| where hb_age_h>24 OR extension_count=0 OR isnull(extension_count)
| stats max(hb_age_h) as max_offline_h by resource_name
| sort -max_offline_h
```

Understanding this SPL

**Azure Arc for Servers Heartbeat and Extension Inventory** ‚ÄĒ Arc is the control path for Azure Policy, Update Management, and hybrid monitoring on HCI nodes. Stale heartbeats or missing extensions mean patches and guest configuration are not enforced, increasing security and uptime risk.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=azure_monitor` `sourcetype="azure:arc:vm"` with fields `resource_name`, `last_heartbeat_epoch`, `agent_version`, `extension_count`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Cloud Services (Azure Monitor / Resource Graph export). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚ÄĒrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: azure_monitor; **sourcetype**: azure:arc:vm. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚ÄĘ Scopes the data: index=azure_monitor, sourcetype="azure:arc:vm", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚ÄĘ `eval` defines or adjusts **hb_age_h** ‚ÄĒ often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
‚ÄĘ Filters the current rows with `where hb_age_h>24 OR extension_count=0 OR isnull(extension_count)` ‚ÄĒ typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚ÄĘ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by resource_name** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
‚ÄĘ Orders rows with `sort` ‚ÄĒ combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 ‚ÄĒ Validate
Compare with the same scope in the vendor HCI or compute console (UCS, vCenter, Nutanix Prism, and so on).

Step 4 ‚ÄĒ Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (stale Arc agents), Single value (machines without extensions), Map (site by resource group).
grandmaExplanation: Arc is the control path for Azure Policy, Update Management, and hybrid monitoring on HCI nodes ‚ÄĒ so hardware faults and capacity problems do not take services down.
knownFalsePositives: Cluster events during planned hardware refresh, capacity adds, or scheduled maintenance modes.
